rationale: The one substantive ground-truth comment (dsocek's) urges deriving the LoRA component list from an existing single source of truth rather than hardcoding, to catch naming-conflict cases generically — X independently makes this exact point, citing pipeline._dit_modules and a corroborated registry.py:427 duplicate list (confirmed by Y's own independent grep), plus adds a concrete, grounded follow-up about the text-encoder gap with actionable fixes. Y explicitly checked for consolidation opportunities and concluded none were justified, directly missing (and effectively contradicting) the reviewer's key concern, leaving only generic 'no issues found' checklist statements with no concrete suggested changes.
